name: TickTick MCP Server
what_it_does: TickTick MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with TickTick task management app — creating and managing tasks and projects, setting reminders and due dates, managing habits, querying inbox and lists, and integrating TickTick's personal productivity platform into agent-driven task automation and personal productivity workflows.
use_cases: Creating tasks and reminders from voice assistant or planning agents
Querying today's tasks and upcoming deadlines from daily planning agents
Managing project lists and organizing work from productivity agents
Setting habits and tracking completion from personal development agents
Generating daily agenda from personal assistant agents
Capturing quick tasks from inbox-processing agents
not_for: Teams using Todoist, Things, or other task managers (use their specific MCPs)
Enterprise project management (TickTick is personal productivity, not team PM)
Teams needing complex workflow automation beyond tasks
best_when: An agent needs to manage personal tasks in TickTick — for personal productivity automation, quick task capture, or daily planning integration.
avoid_when: You're using Todoist, Things, or Notion for task management — or if you need enterprise project management rather than personal task lists.

auth.notes: TickTick OAuth 2.0 with developer app registration. Client ID + secret from TickTick developer portal. Access token for API calls. TickTick developer API requires application approval.

pricing.free_tier_limits: TickTick Free: basic task management, unlimited tasks
pricing.notes: TickTick free tier is generous. Premium adds calendar integration, habit tracking, and advanced features. Community MCP server is open source and free.

known_agent_gotchas: TickTick developer API requires separate app registration and approval
OAuth setup requires TickTick developer account and app creation
TickTick API is not fully public — limited documentation available
Data residency: TickTick servers in US/China — be aware for privacy-sensitive data
Rate limits not publicly documented — implement conservative rate limiting
Community MCP server — may not expose all TickTick features (calendar, Pomodoro, etc.)
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. OAuth 2.0. GDPR. Community MCP server. TickTick data stored in US/China — review for privacy requirements. Protect OAuth tokens. Developer API access requires approval.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. OAuth 2.0. GDPR. Community MCP. Data in US/China — check privacy requirements. Protect OAuth tokens.
